text.title: QA Security Triage Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `QA Security Triage Runbook` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Triage fires on a named cadence (e.g. every Tuesday 14:00 UTC) — not 'when scanner alerts'.
rule.rationale: Without a named cadence, scan output piles up and the queue becomes 'whenever someone has time'.

rule.statement: Every finding has a mapped severity → action (fix-now / fix-this-sprint / accept-with-expiry / suppress-with-rationale).
rule.rationale: Severity without a mapped action is theatre.

rule.statement: Every accept/suppress decision cites: tool ID, scan run, CVE, exploitability assessment, compensating control link.
rule.rationale: Unevidenced acceptances become unauditable findings later.

rule.statement: Every triaged finding has a named human owner (assignee), not a team or channel.
'Accept' decisions carry an expiry date and a re-review trigger. Permanent acceptances are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Permanent acceptance is risk transfer to future-self with no signal.

rule.statement: Quarterly review measures: mean-time-to-fix per severity, expiry-breach count, suppression-overuse count. Failing → revise.
rule.rationale: Without outcome review, triage drifts into noise filtering.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
